transcript.text: Hello, how are you? This is Living in Boulder Vlog, December 21st, 2013. It is Sunday. It's kind of hard to figure out which day because I worked yesterday and I'm working tomorrow.

So, yeah, last night, yeah, I was really tired. I got to sleep really late. I got on my computer and I was listening to YouTube videos about Scientology, all these interviews from ex-people and there's so many of them, dude.
They talk so much. There's so many stories. It's so crazy. It gets crazier and crazier more. You listen to it. I'm going to do a video about it.

But, yeah, so I was up until like 4 in the morning or something. I just had really bad insomnia. So I didn't get up until like 11.30 or something and then my mom went over to her house at like 12.30 to clean up the house a little bit.

And then we were going to go to this play called Dude, It's Boulder that stars a bunch of old people or people from the age of 11 to 92, but a bunch of old people just talking about the history of Boulder.
So it was pretty cool. At the end of it with my mom and a couple of her friends, at the end of it, I went over.
I saw there were a couple of guys filming it with these big tripods and I told them, I was like, yo, you guys going to upload it to YouTube?
And they're like, and then I wrote my number down. I was like, yo, if you guys need anybody to put it up on YouTube, I could do it.
And then the dudes who filmed it were walking by. I'm like, yeah, hey, you guys are going to put it on YouTube?
They're like, yeah, we're going to put it on Vimeo on. Are we going to put it on YouTube?
And they're like, YouTube? I was like, yeah, like, so you put the entire thing on because most people can only do 10 minutes.
He's like, do you have to pay for that? I'm like, no, just some people can do it and some can't.
Anyways, I'll do it. I could do it if you guys want. And they're like, oh, OK, nice to meet you.
So I don't know. Maybe I'll do that. But yeah, then we were driving home and we drove by some house.
And my mom's friend's like, hey, look, there's the Mark and Mindy house because I never actually knew where the Mark and Mindy house was.
Mark and Mindy is that TV show in the 70s starring Robin Williams as the alien Mark.
You can see episodes of it on YouTube. He's all, nannu, nannu.
But and they lived in Boulder. And so, you know, at the beginning of the show, they show the outside of the house where she lives.
This is a beautiful Victorian house. And anyways, yeah.
But my mom's friend lived there in the 70s, like during when they were filming it.
And I was like, yeah. Yeah. Then they like Mindy worked on the New York Deli, which is this deli on Pearl Street, which doesn't exist anymore.
It was there in the 80s. But she's all, yeah, I worked there, too.
And I was like, what? You lived in the Mark and Mindy house and you worked at the New York Deli?
But anyways, I'll film it when I do my video, Boulder. Maybe I'll show where it is.
But then I had dinner over at my mom's house. She had a lot of good food. I had a big old dinner.
And then I came here. Somebody gave me a video request to do a video about Paul Walker who died.

So I was like, all right, I guess I'll rent a Paul Walker movie because I was kind of, didn't feel like going to the gym or riding my bike or anything because it was already like 8.30 and I have to work tomorrow and ride to the next town over to work.
So I rented the Vehicle 19 movie.
But I watched like the first minute of it and then I was like, you know what? I got to watch more of those Scientology interviews.
So I was here working on my, on my Kindle book page and listening to all those Scientology interviews.
I got the movie in the background. Just, just pause because I didn't even watch it. Maybe I'll watch it tomorrow while I'm riding my bike.
But I'd have to move the bike here or maybe I won't watch it.
Oh, today's a yawner, huh? And I'm even tearing up from it.
So that's my day today.
I just took a Trazodone Melatonin Sleepy Time Herbal Pill.
Man, I'm ready to fucking crash, bro.
Because it's like 10.30 or something now.
All right, peace out.
